metadata.name: ARAMCO Cybersecurity Compliance Certification
metadata.description: The ARAMCO Cybersecurity Compliance Certification (CCC) establishes mandatory cybersecurity requirements for all contractors, vendors, and third parties that connect to Saudi Aramco's network, access its systems, or handle its data.
metadata.classification_reasoning: While the ARAMCO CCC has 'certification' in its name, it functions as a compliance requirement for ARAMCO contractors rather than a voluntary industry certification. Contractors must demonstrate compliance to maintain business relationships with Saudi Aramco.
metadata.framework_purpose: To protect Saudi Aramco's information technology and operational technology environments by establishing minimum cybersecurity requirements for all third parties that access Aramco systems, networks, or data.
metadata.framework_scope: All contractors, vendors, suppliers, and third-party service providers that connect to Saudi Aramco networks, access Aramco IT/OT systems, or process/store Aramco data.
framework_objectives: Protect Saudi Aramco's critical infrastructure from cyber threats
Establish minimum security standards for third-party access
Ensure secure handling of Saudi Aramco data
Maintain visibility into third-party security posture
Enable rapid response to security incidents affecting Aramco
metadata.target_audience: CISOs, IT Security Teams, Compliance Officers, and Vendor Managers of Saudi Aramco contractors and suppliers
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in suspension or termination of contracts, exclusion from future projects, and financial penalties as defined in contractual agreements.
metadata.adoption_approach: Third parties must complete a self-assessment, submit evidence of compliance, and may be subject to verification audits by Saudi Aramco or its designated assessors.

controls.title: Information Security Management System
controls.description: Establish and maintain an information security management system.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall establish and maintain an Information Security Management System (ISMS) that addresses the management of cybersecurity risks. The ISMS shall be aligned with internationally recognized standards such as ISO 27001.

controls.title: Security Governance Structure
controls.description: Establish a security governance structure with defined roles and responsibilities.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall establish a security governance structure with clearly defined roles, responsibilities, and accountability for cybersecurity. A senior management representative shall be designated as responsible for information security.

controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures
controls.description: Develop and maintain security policies and procedures.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall develop, maintain, and enforce security policies and procedures covering all aspects of information security. Policies shall be reviewed at least annually and updated as necessary.

controls.title: Security Awareness and Training
controls.description: Implement security awareness and training programs.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement security awareness and training programs for all personnel with access to Saudi Aramco systems or data. Training shall cover security policies, threats, and individual responsibilities.

controls.title: Compliance Reporting
controls.description: Provide compliance reports to Saudi Aramco as required.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall provide compliance reports to Saudi Aramco demonstrating adherence to CCC requirements. Reports shall be submitted according to the schedule defined by Saudi Aramco.

controls.title: Risk Assessment Process
controls.description: Conduct regular risk assessments for Aramco-related systems and data.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall conduct regular risk assessments for systems and data related to Saudi Aramco work. Risk assessments shall identify threats, vulnerabilities, and potential impacts, with risk treatment plans developed.

controls.title: Risk Treatment
controls.description: Implement risk treatment measures for identified risks.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement risk treatment measures for identified risks. Treatment options include risk mitigation through controls, risk transfer, risk acceptance (with justification), or risk avoidance.

controls.title: Third-Party Risk Management
controls.description: Manage cybersecurity risks from subcontractors and fourth parties.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall manage cybersecurity risks from their own subcontractors and fourth parties that may access Saudi Aramco systems or data. Security requirements shall be flowed down to subcontractors.

controls.title: Asset Inventory
controls.description: Maintain an inventory of assets used for Aramco work.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall maintain an accurate inventory of all assets (hardware, software, data) used to process, store, or transmit Saudi Aramco information. Assets shall have assigned owners and be classified.

controls.title: Data Classification and Handling
controls.description: Classify and handle Saudi Aramco data according to its sensitivity.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall classify Saudi Aramco data according to Saudi Aramco's data classification scheme. Appropriate handling, storage, and disposal procedures shall be implemented for each classification level.

controls.title: Secure Data Disposal
controls.description: Securely dispose of Aramco data when no longer required.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall securely dispose of Saudi Aramco data when no longer required for the contracted work. Disposal methods shall render data unrecoverable, with destruction certificates provided upon request.

controls.title: Access Control Policy
controls.description: Implement access control policies based on least privilege.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement access control policies based on the principle of least privilege. Access to Saudi Aramco systems and data shall be limited to personnel with a legitimate business need.

controls.title: User Authentication
controls.description: Implement strong authentication for access to Aramco systems.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement strong authentication mechanisms for access to Saudi Aramco systems and data. Multi-factor authentication shall be required for remote access and privileged accounts.

controls.title: Privileged Access Management
controls.description: Implement controls for privileged access accounts.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement privileged access management controls. Privileged accounts shall be inventoried, minimized, and their use monitored. Just-in-time access shall be implemented where possible.

controls.title: Access Review
controls.description: Conduct regular reviews of access rights.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall conduct regular reviews of access rights to Saudi Aramco systems and data. Reviews shall occur at least quarterly, with unnecessary access promptly revoked.

controls.title: Personnel Offboarding
controls.description: Revoke access immediately upon personnel termination or role change.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall revoke access to Saudi Aramco systems and data immediately upon personnel termination or role change. Offboarding procedures shall ensure complete access removal within 24 hours.

controls.title: Data Encryption
controls.description: Encrypt Saudi Aramco data at rest and in transit.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall encrypt Saudi Aramco data at rest and in transit using strong, industry-accepted encryption algorithms. Encryption shall use AES-256 or equivalent for data at rest and TLS 1.2+ for data in transit.

controls.title: Key Management
controls.description: Implement secure cryptographic key management.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement secure cryptographic key management practices. Keys shall be generated using secure methods, stored securely, rotated periodically, and destroyed when no longer needed.

controls.title: Physical Security Perimeter
controls.description: Implement physical security for facilities with Aramco systems.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement physical security controls for facilities housing systems that process or store Saudi Aramco data. Physical access shall be restricted to authorized personnel with access logging.

controls.title: Equipment Security
controls.description: Protect equipment from unauthorized access and environmental threats.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall protect equipment containing Saudi Aramco data from unauthorized access, theft, and environmental threats. Equipment shall be secured when not in use and during transportation.

controls.title: Secure Work Areas
controls.description: Establish secure work areas for handling Aramco confidential data.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall establish secure work areas for handling Saudi Aramco confidential data. Work areas shall have appropriate access controls, clean desk policies, and screen privacy measures.

controls.title: Change Management
controls.description: Implement change management for systems handling Aramco data.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement change management processes for systems handling Saudi Aramco data. Changes shall be documented, tested, approved, and tracked, with rollback procedures available.

controls.title: Malware Protection
controls.description: Implement malware protection on all systems.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement malware protection on all systems that access Saudi Aramco networks or data. Anti-malware solutions shall be current, with real-time protection and scheduled scans.

controls.title: Vulnerability Management
controls.description: Implement vulnerability management for Aramco-connected systems.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement vulnerability management for systems connected to Saudi Aramco networks or handling Aramco data. Vulnerabilities shall be identified through scanning and remediated based on severity.

controls.title: Patch Management
controls.description: Maintain current security patches on all systems.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall maintain current security patches on all systems connected to Saudi Aramco networks. Critical patches shall be applied within 30 days of release, with emergency patches applied sooner.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring
controls.description: Implement security logging and monitoring.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement security logging and monitoring for systems handling Saudi Aramco data. Logs shall capture security events, be protected from tampering, and retained for at least 12 months.

controls.title: Network Security
controls.description: Implement network security controls for Aramco connectivity.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement network security controls for systems connecting to Saudi Aramco networks. Firewalls, network segmentation, and intrusion detection shall be implemented.

controls.title: Backup and Recovery
controls.description: Implement backup and recovery procedures for Aramco data.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall implement backup and recovery procedures for Saudi Aramco data. Backups shall be performed regularly, stored securely, and tested for recoverability.

controls.title: Secure Configuration
controls.description: Maintain secure configurations for all systems.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall maintain secure configurations for all systems handling Saudi Aramco data. Configuration baselines shall be established based on industry standards and deviations monitored.

controls.title: Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Establish an incident response plan for security incidents.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall establish an incident response plan for security incidents affecting Saudi Aramco data or systems. The plan shall define roles, procedures, and communication protocols.

controls.title: Incident Notification
controls.description: Notify Saudi Aramco of security incidents within 4 hours.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall notify Saudi Aramco of security incidents affecting Aramco data or systems within 4 hours of detection. Initial notification shall include incident type, systems affected, and containment actions.

controls.title: Incident Investigation
controls.description: Conduct thorough investigation of security incidents.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall conduct thorough investigation of security incidents affecting Saudi Aramco. Investigation shall determine root cause, scope of impact, and whether data was compromised.

controls.title: Incident Reporting
controls.description: Provide detailed incident reports to Saudi Aramco.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall provide detailed incident reports to Saudi Aramco within 72 hours of incident closure. Reports shall include timeline, impact assessment, root cause, and corrective actions.

controls.title: Incident Cooperation
controls.description: Cooperate with Saudi Aramco incident response teams.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall cooperate fully with Saudi Aramco incident response teams during investigation and remediation of security incidents. Access to systems, logs, and personnel shall be provided as requested.

controls.title: Lessons Learned
controls.description: Conduct post-incident reviews and implement improvements.
controls.full_text: Third parties shall conduct post-incident reviews for significant security incidents and implement improvements to prevent recurrence. Lessons learned shall be documented and shared with Saudi Aramco.
